pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: CHRO surged from ~13 (prior close) to 22.9 (trigger open) on a credible merger announcement: Ligand Pharmaceuticals' Pelthos Therapeutics subsidiary combining with Channel Therapeutics to commercialize FDA-approved ZELSUVMI. The catalyst is real and publicly disclosed via GlobeNewswire (T3 tier). However, the preponderance of evidence supports a WATCH posture, not entry, due to structural absence of corroborating hard-to-fake signals: (1) No SEC 8-K filing confirmation despite material M&A event that mandates disclosure within 4 business days — this is a critical structural absence for a listed company; (2) Fundamental snapshot entirely null, preventing valuation anchor or pro-forma assessment; (3) Information integrity assessment flags low-tier-only sources (T3=1, T4=2; no T1/T2 primary confirmation); (4) Company is deeply unprofitable (PE negative, net margin -257.93%), recent earnings miss (EPS -0.32 vs -0.21 est); (5) Price action contradicts positive narrative: -10% 5d, -21% 20d, -53% 60d, with volume exhausted at 22% of 20-day average — this is a reversal/exhaustion pattern, not a sustained surge; (6) Wikipedia pageviews show 3 views on announcement day (baseline 0-5 range) — no abnormal retail attention; (7) Analyst sentiment frozen at June 2024 (1 Buy rating), no post-announcement update; (8) Macro regime mandates strict_quality_and_valuation_caution, disfavoring speculative M&A plays. The bear thesis is coherent: the surge is momentum-driven on a headline without underlying value creation clarity, and the absence of SEC corroboration + null fundamentals + negative price action + exhausted volume + low-tier-only information sources = a pump-without-corroboration pattern. The upstream surge_short agent correctly recommends WATCH pending SEC 8-K confirmation and fundamental data. Risk agent confirms no hard-veto conditions tripped. PM decision: WATCH. Rationale: the structural absence of SEC filing confirmation for a material M&A event is itself a bear-thesis signal under §5.17 surge-short integrity logic, but the evidence is not yet sufficient for entry because the merger announcement itself is credible (GlobeNewswire, concrete details). If SEC 8-K is absent 4+ business days post-announcement, escalate to SHORT at 0.005 entry. If 8-K confirms binding merger with clear deal terms and regulatory path, re-evaluate for SHORT entry. Current state: insufficient evidence for entry; recommend monitoring SEC EDGAR and fundamental data population.

surge_short.reasoning_summary: CHRO surged from ~13 (prior close) to 22.9 (trigger open) on a merger announcement: Ligand's Pelthos Therapeutics subsidiary combining with Channel Therapeutics to commercialize FDA-approved ZELSUVMI. The catalyst is real and publicly disclosed via GlobeNewswire (T3 source). However, the short thesis rests on structural absence of corroborating evidence: (1) No SEC 8-K filing confirmation despite a material M&A event that should trigger mandatory disclosure; (2) Fundamental snapshot entirely null, preventing valuation anchor; (3) Information integrity assessment flags low-tier-only sources (T3/T4), uncorroborated status; (4) Company is unprofitable (PE negative, net margin -257.93%), recent earnings miss (EPS -0.32 vs -0.21 est); (5) Macro regime is strict_quality_and_valuation_caution, disfavoring speculative M&A plays; (6) Wikipedia pageviews show minimal abnormal attention (3 views on announcement day, baseline 0-5 range). The merger narrative is positive for Channel (acquisition by larger pharma), but CHRO's identity and role in the combined entity are unclear from the packet. Absence of SEC filing confirmation for a material M&A event is a red flag under surge-short structural-absence logic: a listed company announcing a merger should have filed 8-K within 4 business days. The lack of fundamental data prevents valuation justification. The bear thesis: the surge is momentum-driven on a headline without underlying value creation clarity, and the absence of SEC corroboration suggests either (a) the announcement is incomplete/unconfirmed, or (b) the filing is pending and the market is front-running. Either way, the risk of reversal is elevated.
